title: Sealed Packet II — Cross-Index

sections.heading: RESTRICTED SUMMARY
sections.body:
The public file leaves out the single most damaging contradiction: Charles Decroix's corrected account erases a meeting that appears elsewhere—in a private receipt from a framer, in a porter's log, and in a telegram sent from Sauval's address at twelve-oh-five. Somebody lied before the question was even asked properly. That somebody is not necessarily the killer, but the lie protects someone who was present at a time they claim to have been absent.

Marked reliable by the clerk who was himself twice found mistaken. It was observed at the desk that fear and deceit oft borrow one another's garments. Thus was it lodged in the file, though not one man at the desk would swear to the whole of it. The desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers."

sections.heading: INDEX NOTE
sections.body:
Attached exhibits were re-stamped 62-62 after midnight review. The re-stamp number was not copied to the outer folder. This irregularity suggests that someone with access to the file room wished to bury a particular document—likely the bribery ledger—under a pile of administrative confusion.

Filed in a fair hand; trust it not overmuch. One of the detained souls lies outright, another lies by omission, and a third speaketh true in so crooked a manner that truth itself seems perjured. There is a seam in this account where another hand hath tugged at it. The desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers."

Inspector Lambert Caillou appears in the restricted notes more often than in the open file, but never in the same handwriting twice. Some entries are in Sauval's hand, some in an unknown clerk's, some in a script that matches Caillou's own. The cumulative effect is that of a man trying to erase himself from a narrative that keeps pulling him back in.

Entered as sober truth, though truth oft weareth a counterfeit coat. The constables disputed the matter till dawn: whether the witness erred, dissembled, or sought by falsehood to shield a greater villainy. The phrasing is too neat by half, which in murder-books is seldom a happy omen. The desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers."

Pencilled upon the inward flap, as though it were but a warehouse remembrance: seventh bell, seventh stair, first lantern, fourth berth.

subtitle: Trust this sheet if thou wilt; the sergeant trusted it not, yet kept it.
